title: FOIA US Navy records 5/15 002 Vietnam War

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Military service records for subject below;
Name: Charles Hill
Branch: United States Navy
DOB: 12/01/1934
Dates of Service: Approx 1954 to 1984

Request following documents;
1) Bronze Star Medal award citations
2) History of Assignments NAVPERS form 601-5
3) Navy commendation medal award citation
4) Occupational training history NAVPERS 601-4
5) transfers and receipts NAVPERS 601-12
Historical Documentation
5326.  ST-1
Redact per 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).
I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information.

Please mail hardcopies to:
MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115
or please email pdf copies to requests@muckrock.com

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: SOPs, data requirements, and review templates for TSCA biotech environmental-release submissions

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: SOPs, data requirements, and review templates for TSCA biotech environmental-release submissions
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), desk manuals, review handbooks, or equivalent internal guidance documents used by EPA staff to review TSCA Section 5 submissions for microorganisms intended for environmental release or field testing — including TERAs, MCANs, and Tier I/II exemption notices.

Internal checklists, data requirements tables, or templates identifying the categories of information EPA requests or expects from submitters in connection with environmental-release submissions.

Risk assessment frameworks, templates, or guidance documents used to evaluate environmental-release submissions, including any documents describing how human health, ecological, and environmental risks are characterized for engineered microorganisms.

Templates used to draft consent orders, Significant New Use Rules (SNURs), or "not unreasonable risk" determinations for biotech submissions involving environmental release.
Internal training materials prepared for new reviewers in the Biotechnology Program.

I am not requesting case-specific risk assessments or submitter-specific records in this request — only the generic procedural, methodological, and training documents.
Date range: January 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed.
Format: Electronic copies in original format (PDF, Word, Excel). Please provide records on a rolling basis as they become available.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty

title: Index of TERA, MCAN, and Tier I/II biotech submissions for environmental release, FY2021–present

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Index of TERA, MCAN, and Tier I/II biotech submissions for environmental release, FY2021–present
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list, index, log, tracking spreadsheet, or equivalent record identifying all of the following submissions received by EPA where the submission concerns environmental release, field testing, or any intended use of the microorganism outside of contained structures:

TSCA Experimental Release Applications (TERAs)
Microbial Commercial Activity Notices (MCANs)
Tier I exemption notices
Tier II exemption notices

For each submission, I request the following non-CBI fields to the extent maintained:

Case number or docket identifier
Submission title or short description
Date of receipt
Submitter name (where not designated as CBI)
Type of submission (TERA, MCAN, Tier I, or Tier II)
Status or disposition (e.g., under review, withdrawn, dropped from review, completed, expired)
Date of final EPA action, if any
Intended use category or organism type, where captured in tracking records

I am specifically excluding submissions limited to contained use (e.g., fermentor-based industrial production with no environmental release component).
Date range: October 1, 2020 through the date this request is processed.
If no single tracking record exists, please provide the closest equivalent (e.g., entries exported from the Biotechnology Program's case management system).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty

title: Records schedule and file plan for the OPPT Biotechnology Program

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records schedule and file plan for the OPPT Biotechnology Program
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

...specifically pertaining to the Biotechnology Program within the New Chemicals Division (or successor office responsible for microorganism submissions under TSCA Section 5):
1. The current records control schedule(s), records disposition schedule(s), and file plan(s) governing records created or maintained by the OPPT Biotechnology Program, including any schedules approved by the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA).
2. Any internal guidance, indexes, or documents that identify the categories of records the Biotechnology Program creates or maintains in connection with submissions for microorganisms under TSCA Section 5 — including but not limited to TSCA Experimental Release Applications (TERAs), Microbial Commercial Activity Notices (MCANs), Tier I exemption notices, and Tier II exemption notices.
3. Any records retention or disposal forms (e.g., SF-115 or equivalent) submitted to NARA in the last five years that cover Biotechnology Program records.

Date range: I am requesting current/active schedules and any versions in effect during the past five years.
Format: Electronic copies in their original format (PDF, Word, Excel) are preferred. Please provide records on a rolling basis as they become available rather than waiting for the entire request to be processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Niko McCarty
